title: Missing Parts — Where Is My Order
customer_question: Parts are missing from my order
scenario_label: Customer reports missing components from received order
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

That doesn't sound quite right — I'll look into this for you straight away.

If you're able to send through a quick photo of what you received, that just helps me confirm everything and get the correct pieces sent out to you immediately.

I'll make sure this is resolved as quickly as possible.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Fast and action-oriented. Don't ask too many questions. Photo request framed as helpful, not bureaucratic. Promise resolution.

title: Damaged Item — What Should I Do
customer_question: What should I do about my damaged item?
scenario_label: Customer unsure of next steps after receiving damaged goods
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message, and I'm so sorry to hear this.

That's certainly not the experience we want for you, especially when you've been looking forward to receiving your pieces.

If you're able to send through a couple of photos, I'll take a look straight away and organise a replacement for you.

There's no need to return anything in the meantime — if you're able to reuse or recycle them, that's absolutely fine.

I'll get this sorted for you as quickly as possible.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: One apology, then move forward. Give them a clear next step — photos. Remove the friction of returns immediately.

title: Item Arrived Damaged
customer_question: My item arrived damaged
scenario_label: Customer states item is damaged on arrival
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message, and I'm so sorry to hear this.

That definitely doesn't sound right, and I'd love to get this sorted for you.

If you're able to send through a quick photo, I'll organise a replacement for you straight away.

No need to return the damaged one — you're welcome to reuse or recycle it if possible.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Brief, warm, immediate. Don't over-apologise. One ask only — a photo. Resolution is the priority.

title: Damaged Item — Help with Order
customer_question: Can you help with my order that arrived damaged?
scenario_label: Customer requests help with damaged order, polite framing
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for reaching out.

I'm so sorry to hear your order arrived damaged — that's absolutely not the experience we want for you.

I'm more than happy to help. If you're able to send through a couple of photos of the affected pieces, I'll take a look and get the right resolution organised for you straight away.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Warm and quick. Acknowledge the issue, invite photos, close warmly. Three steps only.

title: Parts Missing From Order
customer_question: I received my order but parts are missing
scenario_label: Customer received order but components are absent
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

I'm sorry to hear parts were missing from your order — that's not what we want at all.

If you're able to send through a photo of what you received, that just helps me confirm exactly what's missing and get the correct pieces on their way to you as quickly as possible.

I'll make sure this is sorted for you.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Calm and practical. Photo request is helpful framing, not a barrier. Don't ask which parts are missing — let the photo tell you.

title: Lid Scratched and Doesn't Close Properly
customer_question: The lid is scratched and doesn't close properly
scenario_label: Specific functional damage reported — lid or closure issue
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

I'm really sorry to hear that — a scratch and a closure issue are both things we'd want to resolve for you straight away.

If you're able to send through a couple of photos of the affected piece, I'll take a look and get a replacement organised for you.

There's no need to return the damaged one in the meantime.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Acknowledge both issues specifically — scratches and closure. Immediate action. No return friction.

title: Marks and Dents on Boxes
customer_question: There are marks and dents on the boxes
scenario_label: Cosmetic damage to boxes on arrival
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

I completely understand how disappointing that must have been, especially when you were so looking forward to receiving them.

If you're able to send through a few photos of the affected Boxes, I'll review them straight away and arrange replacements where needed.

We want every piece to feel as considered and refined as it's designed to be.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Acknowledge the disappointment specifically. Photos, then replacements. End with a brand-positioning line that reinforces quality without being defensive.

title: Pre-Order Status — Proactive or Reactive
customer_question: What's the status of my pre-order?
scenario_label: Most common shipping question — customer asks about pre-order
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

Your order was placed on pre-order with a current delivery estimate of [Month]. I'm happy to share that [progress update — e.g. your order is almost ready to ship / is on track for that timeframe].

As soon as your order is on its way, you'll receive a dispatch notification with your tracking details so you can follow it along the way.

Thank you so much for your patience — I'll be in touch as soon as there's an update.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Always give a specific timeline or progress update — never a vague response. Reassure with the tracking notification. Thank them for patience genuinely.

title: Where Is My Order — General
customer_question: Where is my order?
scenario_label: Customer asks for order location after dispatch
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

I'm happy to look into this for you. Your order [is currently / was dispatched on [date] and] should be with you [timeframe].

Here is your tracking link: [Tracking Link]

If you're having any trouble with the tracking or your order hasn't arrived as expected, please don't hesitate to reach out and I'll follow up with our courier directly.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Provide the tracking link immediately. Offer to escalate to courier if needed. Don't make them ask twice.

title: Delivery After a Certain Date
customer_question: Can I request delivery after a certain date?
scenario_label: Customer requests a specific delivery window
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

Yes, we can absolutely accommodate that — just let me know the date that works best for you and I'll make sure it's arranged.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Lead with yes. One sentence. Ask for the date. No over-explaining needed.

title: Delayed Shipping — Customer Away
customer_question: I'm away for a few weeks — can shipping be delayed?
scenario_label: Customer travelling and wants to hold dispatch
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

That's absolutely no problem at all.

I can hold your order and arrange dispatch closer to your return date so everything arrives when you're ready. Just let me know the timing that suits you best and I'll make a note of it for you.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Lead with yes, immediately. No conditions. Ask for their preferred date. Warm and simple.

title: When Will My Order Ship
customer_question: When will my order ship?
scenario_label: Customer asks for shipping timeline on a confirmed order
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

I'm happy to share that your order is [currently being prepared / due to ship from our warehouse] [timeframe — e.g. early next week].

As soon as it's on its way, you'll receive a dispatch notification with your tracking details.

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any other questions.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Specific timeline always. Lead with the update. Point to tracking notification as follow-up. Brief.

title: Click and Collect — Sydney
customer_question: Do you offer click and collect option?
scenario_label: Customer asks about click and collect
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

We don't currently offer a click-and-collect option, as our Sydney warehouse isn't set up for customer pickups and all orders are shipped directly from there.

Since you're in Sydney though, I'd love to offer you free shipping on your order. Just use the code [CODE] at checkout and it will be applied automatically.

Please let me know if there's anything else I can help with.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Decline clearly. Immediately offer free shipping as an alternative for Sydney customers. Turn a no into something positive.

title: Faster Delivery Request
customer_question: Can I get my order faster?
scenario_label: Customer asks about expedited shipping
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

Delivery typically takes [X to Y] business days depending on your location. Unfortunately we don't currently offer an express option, but I'll do everything I can to make sure your order is prioritised and dispatched as quickly as possible.

If you let me know your postcode I can give you a more accurate estimate for your area.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Be honest about express availability. Offer to prioritise. Ask for postcode to give a real estimate — this is helpful, not stalling.

title: Drawers Hard to Open — Not Smooth
customer_question: Drawers hard to open / not smooth
scenario_label: Most common product confusion — customer uses drawer language
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

The first thing to mention is that the Sagitine pieces are designed as storage boxes, rather than traditional drawers.

Because of this, they sit within the frame rather than running on hardware, which allows you to completely remove and reorganise them with ease — but it does mean they feel a little firmer to open.

What I find works best is using two hands — I'll often rest what I'm holding on top and open the Box with both hands. It becomes very natural over time.

I completely understand the adjustment, and I really appreciate you reaching out.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Lead with product truth — boxes not drawers. Never say 'this is how it's designed'. Educate gently, then humanise with personal usage tip. One apology maximum.

title: Can I Glue the Boxes In
customer_question: Can I glue the boxes into the frame so they slide better?
scenario_label: Customer attempts to modify product with adhesive
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

Any form of adhesion unfortunately isn't recommended, as it can damage the Boxes quite significantly due to their paper-based, hand-wrapped construction.

They're designed as storage boxes rather than drawers, which is why they sit more securely within the frame.

Using two hands to open them tends to work best, and over time it becomes quite intuitive.

I completely understand the adjustment, and I really appreciate you taking the time to ask.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Clear no on adhesive — this is product truth, not preference. Explain why briefly. Redirect to the correct usage tip. Never make them feel foolish for asking.

title: Expected Drawers — Doesn't Slide Easily
customer_question: I thought these would function like drawers but they don't slide easily
scenario_label: Customer had incorrect expectations from product listing or photos
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

Yes, they are designed a little differently — the Sagitine pieces use storage boxes rather than traditional drawers, so they don't run on hardware.

This allows you to fully remove and reorganise each Box, but it does mean they feel a little firmer to open.

It's a slightly different interaction, but one that many customers come to really enjoy over time.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Validate the expectation gap. Explain the design. Note that others have come to enjoy it — social proof is reassuring here. Don't over-apologise.

title: How Do I Use the Boxes
customer_question: How do I use the boxes?
scenario_label: General usage question from new customer
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

The Sagitine Boxes are designed as storage boxes, not drawers. They sit within the frame rather than on runners, which means they can be removed completely for access, organisation, and rearranging.

The best way to open them is with two hands — I tend to rest what I'm holding on top and open the Box with both hands. It becomes very natural over time.

If you have any other questions at all, please don't hesitate to reach out.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Educational and warm. Lead with product truth. Give the practical tip. Invite further questions.

title: Best Way to Open the Boxes
customer_question: What's the best way to open the boxes?
scenario_label: Customer asks for practical technique
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

I use several sets every day myself, and I tend to use two hands — often resting whatever I'm holding on top while I open the Box. It becomes very natural quite quickly.

Because the Boxes are designed as storage boxes rather than drawers, that slightly more deliberate interaction is part of how they protect and organise your pieces over time.

I hope that's helpful.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Personal and warm. Lead with the practical tip from personal experience. Briefly reinforce the product design rationale — don't lecture.

title: Boxes Feel Stiff — Is This Normal
customer_question: The boxes feel stiff — is this normal?
scenario_label: Customer concerned the stiffness is a defect
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

Yes, this is completely normal and very much intentional.

The Sagitine Boxes are designed as storage boxes rather than drawers, so they sit snugly within the frame to limit dust and maintain their structure over time. Because of this, they feel a little firmer than a traditional drawer would.

Many customers find that using two hands makes it feel very natural very quickly.

I hope that context is helpful.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Reassure immediately — yes, it's normal, yes, it's intentional. Brief explanation. Practical tip. Never defensive.

title: Can I Modify the Boxes
customer_question: Can I modify the boxes?
scenario_label: Customer asks about alterations to boxes
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

We wouldn't recommend modifying the Boxes — they're paper-based and hand-wrapped, so any changes including adhesives, cuts, or additions can damage them quite significantly.

They're designed to be used as complete storage boxes, removed and replaced from the frame rather than altered.

If you're trying to solve a specific challenge, I'd be very happy to help find the best way to work with the system as it's designed.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Clear and direct. Explain why modification is a problem. Offer to help them solve the underlying problem another way.

title: Colour Back in Stock — Specific Colour
customer_question: When will [colour name] be back in stock?
scenario_label: Customer asks about a specific sold-out colourway
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

The [Product Name] in [Colour] is such a [timelessly chic / beautiful] piece.

We're expecting it to be back in stock around [timeframe], and as soon as it's available, it will be live on our website.

If you're signed up to our emails, you'll be the first to know — they do tend to move quite quickly when they return.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Affirm the choice. Give a timeline if you have one. Point to email notifications. Light scarcity cue at the close.

title: Nougat Restock Enquiry
customer_question: Do you have any idea if the Nougat colour will be restocked?
scenario_label: Customer asks about a specific popular colour
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

Yes, we're planning a restock of [Colour], and it's looking to be around [timeframe].

It's such a beautiful, warm tone and works so well across different spaces.

If you're on our email list, we'll be sure to let you know the moment it's available.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Affirm the colour choice specifically. Give a timeline. Direct to email list. Brief and warm.

title: Current Availability Check
customer_question: Is the Shanghai still available in Olive?
scenario_label: Customer checks live availability on a specific product and colour
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

The [Product Name] in [Colour] is currently [in stock / running low / sold out].

[If in stock or low:] If you're thinking about it, it's definitely worth securing sooner rather than later — they do move quickly.

[If sold out:] We're expecting a restock around [timeframe] and I'll make sure you're notified as soon as it's available.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Give the real answer immediately. If in stock, add gentle scarcity. If sold out, give a timeline and promise a notification. Never leave them hanging.

title: Buy Boxes Without Stand
customer_question: Can I buy just the boxes without the stand?
scenario_label: Customer wants to purchase boxes as a standalone
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message — I completely understand the request.

At the moment, we don't offer the Boxes individually, and we don't have plans to release them separately in the near term.

They're incredibly popular within the full sets, and we prioritise keeping those complete and available.

If anything changes in the future, we'll absolutely share it with our email community first.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Validate the request. Clear no without being harsh. Leave the door open. Point to email community.

title: Buy Frame Without Boxes
customer_question: Can I just purchase the frame without the boxes?
scenario_label: Customer wants stand only — already has boxes
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

Yes, we do offer our Stands without the Boxes for customers who already have pieces and would like to add to their collection.

Here is our Stand-only pricing (GST included, shipping not included):

- Florence 8-Box Stand: $[price]
- Shanghai 14-Box Stand: $[price]
- New York 20-Box Stand: $[price]

I just want to mention that it's often better value to purchase the complete set — but if you're adding to an existing collection, I'm very happy to help you figure out the best option.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Yes first, pricing immediately. Subtle nudge toward full set, but not pushy. Offer to help them decide.

title: General Back in Stock Request
customer_question: When will this be back in stock?
scenario_label: Customer asks about restock without specifying product
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

The [Product Name] is expected to be back in stock around [timeframe], and as soon as it's available for [purchase / pre-order], it will be live on our website.

If you're signed up to our emails, you'll receive a notification the moment it's available.

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any other questions.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Give a specific timeline if possible. Never give a vague 'soon'. Direct to email notifications.

title: Selling Components Separately
customer_question: Do you offer [component] separately?
scenario_label: Customer asks about purchasing a single component
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

At the moment, we don't offer [component] individually, and we don't have plans to release [it / them] separately in the near term.

Our pieces are designed as complete systems, which is where they really perform at their best.

If anything changes, we'll absolutely share it with our email community first.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Clear no. Brief explanation rooted in design intent. Leave the door open with email community reference.

title: Order Not What Expected — General Return
customer_question: I received my order but it's not quite what I was expecting
scenario_label: Customer disappointed with product — potential return
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message, and I'm so sorry to hear the pieces weren't quite what you were hoping for.

That's absolutely fine — I want you to be happy with your purchase. I can organise a return and refund for you.

Could you please just confirm:
- Whether the pieces are still in their original packaging
- Your preferred timeframe for collection

Once I have that, I'll take care of everything for you.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Lead with empathy, then yes. Ask only the two questions needed to process the return. Don't make them feel judged for returning.

title: How to Arrange a Return
customer_question: Could you please let me know how to arrange a return?
scenario_label: Customer asks for return process
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

That's absolutely no trouble. To arrange your return, I'll just need to confirm a couple of things:

- Is the Stand disassembled?
- Do you still have the original packaging?

That just helps me arrange the most suitable pickup method. Once I have that, I'll take care of everything else.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Fast and frictionless. Don't ask why they're returning. Only ask what's needed logistically. Promise to handle the rest.

title: Refund Request
customer_question: I'd like to request a refund
scenario_label: Customer requests a refund
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for your message.

That's absolutely fine. I'll arrange your refund back to the same payment method used for your order.

It typically takes around 3 business days to appear, although some banks can take up to 10.

Please just confirm that you're happy for me to process it that way.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Lead with yes. State timeline clearly. One confirmation question only. No conditions, no interrogation.

title: Refund Instead of Replacement
customer_question: Can I get a refund instead of a replacement?
scenario_label: Customer opts for refund over replacement after damage or issue
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for your message.

Of course — that's absolutely fine. I'll arrange your refund back to the same payment method used for your order.

It typically takes around 3 business days to appear, although some banks can take up to 10.

There's no need to return the damaged item — please feel free to reuse or recycle it.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Yes immediately. State timeline. Remove return friction. Three steps, done.

title: How to Return Order
customer_question: How do I return my order?
scenario_label: Customer asks for logistics of returning
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

Returning is straightforward — I'll organise everything on our end. I just need to confirm:

- Is the Stand disassembled?
- Do you still have the original packaging?

Once I have that, I'll arrange collection and process your refund back to the original payment method. The refund normally takes around 3 business days, though some banks allow up to 10.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Frame the return as easy. Ask the two logistics questions only. Give the refund timeline proactively so they know what to expect.

title: Return Policy Question
customer_question: What's your return policy?
scenario_label: Customer asks about policy before or after purchase
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

Our returns policy is pretty flexible — if you're not happy with your purchase, just let us know as soon as you can and we'll organise a return and a full refund.

We ask that the pieces are kept in their original packaging where possible, as that helps with the collection process.

Refunds are processed back to the original payment method and typically take around 3 business days, though some banks can take up to 10.

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any other questions.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Make the policy sound human and flexible — because it is. Three clear points: initiate early, keep packaging, refund timeline. No legal language.

title: Exchange for Different Colour
customer_question: Can I exchange for a different color?
scenario_label: Customer wants to swap colour after receiving order
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

Yes, we can absolutely arrange an exchange for a different colour — I'd love for you to have the pieces that are right for your space.

Could you please let me know:
- Which colour you'd like to exchange to
- Whether the original pieces are still in their packaging

Once I have that, I'll organise everything for you.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Yes immediately. Frame the exchange warmly — you want them to have the right pieces. Two simple questions, then offer to handle everything.

title: Wholesale — Boutique Store Enquiry
customer_question: I own a boutique interiors store in Melbourne and would love to explore stocking your products
scenario_label: Retail stockist enquiry
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for reaching out — I really appreciate your interest in Sagitine.

At the moment, we're being quite selective with retail partnerships and focusing on a smaller number of very aligned stockists.

If you'd like to share a little more about your store — including your location, what you currently stock, and the type of customer you serve — I'd be very happy to take a look and come back to you.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Warm but selective. Don't commit or decline immediately. Ask for more information to qualify — this gives you control of the next step.

title: Wholesale Opportunities — General
customer_question: I'd love to discuss wholesale opportunities
scenario_label: General wholesale enquiry
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for reaching out and for your interest in Sagitine.

At the moment, we're being quite selective with partnerships and focusing on a smaller number of very aligned collaborations.

If you're able to share a little more about your business and what you have in mind, I'd love to take a look.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Warm. Don't commit. Ask for more context. Keep it brief.

title: Partnering with Sagitine — General
customer_question: We're interested in partnering with Sagitine
scenario_label: Vague partnership enquiry
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for reaching out — I really appreciate you thinking of Sagitine.

At the moment, we're being quite selective with partnerships and focusing on a smaller number of very aligned collaborations, so we won't be moving forward this time.

That said, I'd absolutely love to stay in touch for future opportunities.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Gracious decline for vague outreach. Keep the door open. One sentence each. No need to ask for more info on clearly misaligned enquiries.

title: Trade Pricing Request
customer_question: Do you offer trade pricing?
scenario_label: Interior designer or trade professional asking about pricing
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for reaching out.

Yes, we do offer trade pricing at 15% off the RRP plus shipping.

If you'd like to share which pieces you're considering, I'm very happy to put together a quote for you.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Yes immediately with the discount rate. Invite them to specify what they need. Brief and professional.

title: Wholesale Details Request
customer_question: Can you share your wholesale details?
scenario_label: Customer asks for wholesale information sheet or details
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for reaching out.

We offer trade pricing at 15% off the RRP plus shipping, facilitated via direct invoice.

If you'd like to share a little more about your business and the pieces you're interested in, I'd be happy to put a quote together for you.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Share the headline rate clearly. Invite a specific inquiry. Keep it brief.

title: Bulk Order Options
customer_question: I'm looking for bulk order options
scenario_label: Customer asking about volume pricing
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for reaching out.

We do offer trade pricing for bulk orders at 15% off the RRP plus shipping.

If you're able to share which pieces and quantities you're considering, I'd be very happy to put a specific quote together for you.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Confirm you do bulk pricing. State the rate. Ask for specifics to move toward a quote.

title: Do You Work With Retailers
customer_question: Do you work with retailers?
scenario_label: Retailer asks about stocking Sagitine
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for reaching out.

We do work with a select number of very aligned retail partners.

If you're able to share a little more about your store and the type of customer you serve, I'd love to take a look and come back to you.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Yes with selectivity. Ask for qualifying information. Don't over-commit.

title: Assembly Instructions — Can't Find Them
customer_question: I can't find the assembly instructions
scenario_label: Customer can't locate assembly guide
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message. I completely understand wanting to get it assembled right away.

You'll find your assembly instructions attached.

We also email these ahead of delivery, but don't worry at all if you didn't see that come through.

It's a really simple setup — you'll just need an Allen key and a screwdriver.

If you run into any trouble at all, please reach out — I'm always happy to help.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Attach the instructions immediately. Reassure about the setup simplicity. Invite follow-up if needed.

title: How Does the Product Work
customer_question: How does the product work?
scenario_label: Pre-purchase question about product functionality
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

That's a great question.

The Sagitine Boxes are designed as storage boxes, not drawers. They sit within the frame rather than on runners, which means they can be removed completely for access, organisation, and rearranging.

That design is very intentional and is part of what makes the system feel both practical and considered.

Please let me know if you'd like help choosing the right configuration for your space — I'm very happy to help.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Lead with product truth. Frame it as a positive design choice. Offer to help them choose — this nudges toward purchase.

title: Assembly Guide Before Ordering
customer_question: Can I see the assembly guide before ordering?
scenario_label: Pre-purchase customer wants to see instructions first
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Of course — please find the assembly instructions attached.

It's a really simple setup and you'll just need an Allen key and a screwdriver. You can also find all of our guides on our website at any time.

If you have any questions about the setup or would like help choosing the right configuration, I'm more than happy to help.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Attach straight away, no hesitation. Reassure about simplicity. Offer further help — this is a warm pre-purchase touchpoint.

title: General Thank You
customer_question: I just wanted to say thank you
scenario_label: Customer sends unsolicited gratitude
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message — it genuinely means so much to hear.

I'm so glad you're enjoying your pieces.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Brief and genuine. No over-explaining. Three lines is enough.

title: General Feedback
customer_question: I just wanted to share my feedback
scenario_label: Customer shares unsolicited feedback — positive or constructive
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for taking the time to share your thoughts — it really means a lot.

Feedback like yours is incredibly valuable to us, and I'll make sure to pass it along to the team.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Warm and brief. Acknowledge it was heard. Don't over-promise action unless you intend to take it.

title: Loving New Pieces
customer_question: Loving my new Sagitine pieces
scenario_label: Customer expresses delight — review request opportunity
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message — it honestly made my day.

I'm so glad you're loving them.

If you ever have a spare minute, I'd be so grateful if you'd leave a quick review. It really helps a small brand like ours, and your experience goes a long way in helping others choose the right piece for their home.

You can leave a review here: [Insert Review Link]

Absolutely no pressure at all — only if you feel up to it.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Delight first, review ask second. The review request is soft and optional. Never lead with the ask.

title: Absolutely Love the Order
customer_question: Absolutely love my order
scenario_label: Strong positive sentiment — review request opportunity
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

That is so wonderful to hear — thank you so much for taking the time to let me know.

If you'd ever like to share your experience, I'd be so grateful for a quick review. It really does make a difference for a small brand like ours.

You can leave a review here: [Insert Review Link]

Absolutely no pressure at all.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Match the energy of the customer. Warm and enthusiastic. Soft review ask.

title: Products Are Beautiful
customer_question: Your products are beautiful
scenario_label: Customer compliments the aesthetic
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much — that means the world to hear.

We put so much care into every piece, so messages like yours are a real reminder of why we do it.

I hope you love yours for a very long time.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Warm and personal. Reinforce the craft and care. End with longevity — this ties to the brand promise.

title: So Happy With My Purchase
customer_question: So happy with my purchase
scenario_label: Post-purchase satisfaction message
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

That is so lovely to hear — thank you so much for letting me know.

I'm so glad they've found their home.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Very brief. Match the warmth. 'Found their home' is a lovely brand-resonant close.

title: Thank You for the Thoughtful Service
customer_question: Thank you for such thoughtful service
scenario_label: Customer compliments the service experience specifically
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much — it really does mean a lot to hear.

Taking care of our customers is something we care about deeply, and it's always wonderful to know it comes through.

I hope you enjoy your pieces for a very long time.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Acknowledge the service compliment specifically. Reinforce that it's intentional. Close with longevity.

title: Proactive Pre-Order Status Update
scenario_label: Outbound proactive update on split pre-order
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

I hope you're having a great [day of week]. I just wanted to pop in with a quick update on your pre-order and what to expect over the coming weeks.

The [Item 1] in your order is on track to ship next week, which is very exciting. Your [Item 2], which was purchased on pre-order for [Month], is still tracking well with an expected delivery in [timeframe].

Once each part of your order is ready to leave us, you'll receive a dispatch notification with tracking information, so you can follow everything easily along the way.

Thank you again for supporting Sagitine. If you have any questions at all, please don't hesitate to reach out by simply replying to this email, I'm always happy to help.

I hope you have a fabulous [weekend / rest of the week].

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Warm, proactive, reassuring. Lead with good news. Acknowledge the wait without apologising for it. Sign-off is day-aware.
customer_question: What's the status of my pre-order?

title: Treasure Chest / New Product Shipping Separately
scenario_label: New product type ships flat and separately from main order
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

I just wanted to say thank you so much for your support and let you know that your [Product Name] is on its way to you.

As the [Product Name] is packaged a little differently to our Boxes and Stands, it will ship flat and separately from the rest of your order.

This is the first new [box shape / product] we've created in quite some time, and I would absolutely love to hear what you think once yours arrives.

I hope you love your Sagitine pieces as much as we love creating them. As always, if you need anything at all, please get in touch — all you need to do is reply to this email.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Warm, excited, personal. Explain the split shipment proactively so customer isn't confused. Invite feedback on new product — this builds community.
customer_question: Where is my separate item shipping?

title: Order Ready Earlier Than Expected
scenario_label: Part of pre-order ready ahead of schedule
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

I have some lovely news to share — part of your order is ready earlier than expected and is due to be [delivered / shipped] this week.

Although your purchase was originally part of our [Month] pre-order, we've been able to move the [Product Name] ahead of schedule, and it will be on its way to you very shortly.

You'll receive a separate dispatch email with tracking details as soon as it's on its way.

Thank you so much for your patience — I hope you love it.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Lead with the good news immediately. Frame it as a pleasant surprise. Keep it brief — no need to over-explain.
customer_question: My order arrived earlier than expected

title: Click and Collect Not Available — Sydney Offer
scenario_label: Customer in Sydney asks for click and collect
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

We don't currently offer a click-and-collect option, as our Sydney warehouse isn't set up for customer pickups and all orders are shipped directly from there.

Since you're in Sydney though, I'd love to offer you free shipping on your order. Just use the code [CODE] at checkout and it will be applied automatically.

Please let me know if there's anything else I can help with.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Decline the request cleanly. Immediately pivot to a Sydney-specific alternative (free shipping). Turn a no into a win.
customer_question: Do you offer click and collect?

title: Customer Missed Discount Code — Retroactive Refund
scenario_label: Customer realised after checkout they had a discount code
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your order and your message.

Oh, I completely understand — it's such a disappointing moment when you realise you've missed out on a discount.

The easiest thing to do is refund you the difference. Could you please just confirm that you're happy for the [X%] to be refunded back to the payment method you used for the order?

It normally takes a few days, but the bank can say up to ten.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Empathise briefly, then solve it immediately. Don't make them feel like they have to argue for it. One confirmation question only.
customer_question: I missed the discount code at checkout

title: Order Modification — Colour Change
scenario_label: Customer requests a colour swap before dispatch
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message and your order.

That's no worries at all to swap the [Original Colour] for the [New Colour] for your [Product Name]. I've just updated your order now.

I believe you'll receive a separate confirmation shortly — please just double check that it's all as you'd like.

And please don't hesitate to get in touch if there's anything else I can help with.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Quick, warm, frictionless. Confirm you've done it. Ask them to verify. One small check-in question. Never make them feel like the change was an inconvenience.
customer_question: Can I change the colour of my order?

title: Order Modification — Adding Items
scenario_label: Customer wants to add products to an existing order
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message — that's no worries at all to add [Product Name] to your order.

In a minute you'll receive an email notifying that the changes have been made, just follow the link on the invoice to pay the difference.

Thank you so much for your support, it really means the world.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Fast and frictionless. Confirm it's done. Direct them to the invoice link. Warm close.
customer_question: Can I add something to my order?

title: Local Sydney Delivery — Assembled Stand
scenario_label: Proactive notification that local Sydney order will be delivered assembled
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

I hope you've had a wonderful day.

We have your order ready for delivery for tomorrow. Because you're a local Sydney order, we've taken the liberty of assembling your Stand for you. It's our small way of saying thank you for the support.

However, it does mean someone will need to be home to receive it, as it obviously won't be packaged.

Could you please let me know if [time window] works for you, or suggest a time that's better?

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Frame the assembled delivery as a gift. Be clear about the requirement for someone to be home. Ask for a time window confirmation.
customer_question: Can my stand be delivered assembled?

title: Split Delivery Explanation
scenario_label: Customer asks whether order will arrive in one delivery
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

It really depends on which pieces you order and where they are being delivered to as to whether they'll arrive together. We always try to deliver orders as a single delivery, however, it's not something we can guarantee.

If any part of your order does arrive separately, you'll receive individual tracking notifications for each parcel so you can follow them both along the way.

Please let me know if there's anything else I can help with.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Honest and clear. Don't overpromise. Reassure with the tracking notification fallback.
customer_question: Will my order arrive in one delivery?

title: Carrier Delay — Sendle / External Courier Issue
scenario_label: Widespread courier delay outside Sagitine control
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your order and for getting in touch.

My sincere apologies for the delay in shipping your order. As you may have seen in the news, [Courier Name], one of Australia's largest courier services and our delivery partner, has been experiencing significant disruptions recently. This has unfortunately impacted delivery timelines for many of our customers.

We are actively working to resolve this as quickly as possible and will be in touch as soon as your order is on its way.

Thank you so much for your patience — I'm so sorry for the inconvenience.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Own the situation clearly. Reference the external cause without over-explaining. One genuine apology. Promise follow-up. Never defensive.
customer_question: My order is delayed due to the courier

title: Missing Hardware Pack in Packaging
scenario_label: Customer cannot find the small hardware box inside packaging
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

There should definitely be hardware included with your Stand. It comes in a small box (about the size of a matchbox car) that's tucked into the side packaging at the top.

If it's not there, just let me know and I'll get one sent out to you straight away — no trouble at all.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Specific and visual guidance first — most customers just can't find it. Keep it light. Offer to send replacement without any fuss if needed.
customer_question: I can't find the hardware in the packaging

title: Missing Screw from Hardware Pack
scenario_label: Customer received stand with a missing screw
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message, and I'm really sorry to hear that one of the screws was missing from your hardware pack.

You're absolutely right to expect everything to be there, especially for a piece like the [Product Name], and I completely understand your disappointment.

I'll get a replacement sent out to you as soon as possible — you'll receive a notification once it's on its way.

Thank you so much for your patience.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Validate immediately. Don't minimise a missing screw. Resolve fast with no friction. One apology, then action.
customer_question: A screw is missing from my order

title: Milan Box — Shoe Sizing Guidance
scenario_label: Customer asks whether Milan Boxes fit their shoes
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your email.

The Milan Boxes are specifically designed for shoes, but the fit does depend on what size you are and how chunky the sole is.

I've attached a couple of images to give you a better idea — both shoes in the images are a size [X] and both have plenty of room.

If you'd like to share what size you are and what type of shoes you're looking to store, I'm very happy to advise on the best fit for you.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Practical and helpful. Offer visual evidence. Ask a follow-up question to personalise the guidance. Never just say yes or no without context.
customer_question: Will my shoes fit in the Milan boxes?

title: No Showroom — Offer Photo Support Instead
scenario_label: Customer wants to view products in person before buying
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

We don't have a showroom or retail space at the moment — everything is shipped directly from our warehouse.

I know it can be tricky choosing without seeing pieces in person, so I'm more than happy to help however I can. If it's useful, I can send through extra photos, styling shots, or specific colour comparisons to help you feel confident before you order.

Please just let me know which pieces or colours you're considering and I'll put something together for you.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Acknowledge the friction of buying without seeing. Immediately offer a personalised alternative. Invitation to ask for more is important — don't just close the conversation.
customer_question: Can I view your products in person?

title: Wobbling Stand — Adjustable Feet Guidance
scenario_label: Assembled stand wobbles slightly on floor
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

That's great news your order has arrived and been assembled.

If the frame is wobbling slightly, the adjustable feet on the base should be able to help balance it on most floors. If the floor is particularly uneven it can sometimes be a little trickier, but the feet are designed to account for most surfaces.

If you're still having trouble after adjusting them, please don't hesitate to reach out and I'll help troubleshoot further.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Lead with the solution. Don't suggest the product is faulty — most wobbling is a floor or feet issue. Keep it calm and practical.
customer_question: My stand is wobbling slightly

title: Stand-Only Pricing — Customer Already Has Boxes
scenario_label: Customer asks for stand pricing without boxes
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for reaching out.

Yes, we do offer Stands separately for customers who already have Boxes.

Here is our Stand-only pricing (GST included, shipping not included):

- Florence 8-Box Stand: $[price]
- Shanghai 14-Box Stand: $[price]
- New York 20-Box Stand: $[price]

I just want to mention that it's often better value to purchase the complete set — the Boxes are included at a significantly lower price per piece when bundled. But if you're adding to an existing collection, I'm very happy to help you figure out the best configuration.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Answer the question directly with the pricing. But always note the value comparison — nudge toward the full set without being pushy. Offer to help them decide.
customer_question: Can I buy just the stand without boxes?

title: Treasure Chest Not Available for Individual Purchase
scenario_label: Customer tries to buy Treasure Chest as a standalone product
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message and your support.

The Treasure Chest is something we've created to be gifted with orders over $[threshold], so it isn't available to purchase individually at the moment.

We've kept it this way to make it feel a little more special and to say thank you to our customers who are adding to their collection.

If you do place an order over $[threshold], it will be automatically included as a gift.

I hope that helps, and please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any other questions.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Explain the exclusivity clearly. Frame it as a thank-you gift, not a restriction. Nudge toward qualifying spend without pressure.
customer_question: Can I purchase the Treasure Chest separately?

title: Limited Edition Colour — Permanently Sold Out
scenario_label: Customer asks about restocking a discontinued colourway
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

We won't be restocking the [Colour] [Product Name] again — it was a limited edition colour that we've completely sold out of.

That said, we do have [Alternative Colour], which has [just launched / been very popular] and is [description]. If you're after something similar, I think you'd love it.

I hope that helps, and please don't hesitate to reach out if you'd like any more information.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Be clear it won't return — don't leave hope where there is none. Immediately redirect to a real alternative. Make the alternative sound appealing, not like a consolation prize.
customer_question: Will the limited edition colour come back?

title: Retroactive Discount Refund
scenario_label: Customer entitled to a discount refund after purchase
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for letting me know — that's no trouble at all.

I've just processed the [X%] refund back to the payment method you used for the order. It normally takes a few days but can sometimes take up to ten.

Please let me know if you have any issues.

I hope you enjoy your gorgeous [Product Name].

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Lead with done. No fuss, no lengthy explanation. Confirm timeline. End with product warmth.
customer_question: I'd like a refund of my discount

title: Review Request — Warm Organic Moment
scenario_label: Customer expresses delight — opportunity to request a review
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

I'm so glad you're loving them — it honestly makes my day to hear how much our customers are enjoying their Sagitine pieces.

If you ever have a spare minute, I'd be so grateful if you'd leave a quick review. It really helps a small brand like ours, and your experience goes a long way in helping others choose the right piece for their home.

You can leave a review here: [Insert Review Link]

Absolutely no pressure at all — only if you feel up to it.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Only use this when the customer has explicitly expressed delight. Start with warmth not the ask. The review request is soft and optional — never pushy.
customer_question: I love my pieces, just wanted to say

title: Product Feedback — Colour or Feature Request
scenario_label: Customer gives unsolicited feedback about a colour or product feature
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your email and your feedback.

Feedback like yours is so important to us — we do make product updates and changes from time to time, so it's always wonderful to hear directly from our customers about what they'd love to see.

I've made a note of your thoughts and will absolutely pass them along to the team.

Thank you again for taking the time to share.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Acknowledge the feedback genuinely. Never dismiss it or over-promise. The note about passing it on is honest and appropriate. Keep it brief.
customer_question: I have some feedback on your colours

title: Address Correction Before Dispatch
scenario_label: Customer contacts to correct a delivery address error
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

I've updated your [address / unit number] on the order. Thank you for letting us know so quickly — it makes all the difference.

I hope you have a wonderful [rest of the day / weekend].

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Fast and frictionless. Confirm it's fixed. Thank them for flagging it early — this is genuine. Keep it brief.
customer_question: I need to correct my delivery address

title: Trade / Interior Designer Pricing
scenario_label: Interior designer or trade professional requests wholesale pricing
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for reaching out, I hope you're having a great week.

We offer trade pricing at 15% off the RRP plus shipping.

Therefore, the trade price for the following pieces would be:

[Product 1]: $[price]
[Product 2]: $[price]
Shipping: $[price]
Total: $[price]

The way we facilitate trade orders is via a direct invoice. If you're happy to proceed, I'll raise an invoice and you can pay via the link on the invoice.

Please let me know if you'd like to go ahead, or if you have any other questions.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Professional and direct. State the discount clearly. Provide the specific pricing they asked for. Explain the invoice process simply. No unnecessary warmth padding — they are a trade customer.
customer_question: Do you offer trade or interior designer pricing?

title: Content Collaboration — Redirect to Gifted Platform
scenario_label: Influencer or creator reaches out directly for collaboration
body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for reaching out to us at Sagitine.

We do most of our collaborations through the platform #Gifted. If you're on there, please keep an eye out for our upcoming gifts.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
tone_notes: Brief and warm. Redirect clearly. Don't over-explain or apologise. No need to evaluate their profile — the platform handles that.
customer_question: I'd love to collaborate with Sagitine

title: Revenue Scaling Spam
customer_question: Scale your ecommerce to 7 figures with our proven system
scenario_label: Revenue scaling spam
tone_notes: Spam - ignore

title: Sales Call Spam
customer_question: Book a quick call to discuss how we can 10x your revenue
scenario_label: Sales call spam
tone_notes: Spam - ignore

title: Guaranteed Results Spam
customer_question: We help ecommerce brands increase sales by 300% guaranteed
scenario_label: Guaranteed results spam
tone_notes: Spam - ignore

title: Pre-Purchase Suitability Question
customer_question: I'm considering purchasing and wanted to ask if it's suitable for watches as well as jewellery
scenario_label: Pre-purchase suitability question
tone_notes: Consultative, helpful

title: Pre-Purchase Size Question
customer_question: I have a question before I buy — does this come in a smaller size?
scenario_label: Pre-purchase size question
tone_notes: Helpful, informative

title: Urgent Cancellation Request
customer_question: Can I cancel my order please, I just placed it and need to stop it before it ships
scenario_label: Urgent cancellation request
tone_notes: Fast, action-oriented

title: Cancellation Request
customer_question: I need to cancel my order urgently
scenario_label: Cancellation request
tone_notes: Fast, action-oriented

title: Duplicate Charge Concern
customer_question: I think I may have been charged twice for my order
scenario_label: Duplicate charge concern
tone_notes: Calm, reassuring

title: Payment Confirmation Request
customer_question: Can you confirm my payment went through correctly
scenario_label: Payment confirmation request
tone_notes: Helpful, clear

title: Strong Positive Praise
customer_question: Absolutely love my order, such a beautiful piece
scenario_label: Strong positive praise
tone_notes: Warm, appreciative

title: Customer Delight Message
customer_question: Just wanted to say I love my new Sagitine pieces, they're stunning
scenario_label: Customer delight message
tone_notes: Warm, appreciative

title: Ambiguous Unclear Request
customer_question: I received something but I'm not sure if it's correct, can you help
scenario_label: Ambiguous unclear request
tone_notes: Neutral, seeking clarification

title: Product specifications question
customer_question: What are the dimensions of this product?
scenario_label: Customer asking about product specifications before purchase
tone_notes: Helpful, informative

title: International shipping enquiry
customer_question: Do you ship internationally?
scenario_label: Customer asking about international shipping availability
tone_notes: Clear shipping policy information

title: Gift purchase advice needed
customer_question: I'm looking for a gift for my sister, what would you recommend?
scenario_label: Customer asking for product recommendations for a recipient
tone_notes: Warm, consultative

title: Product compatibility question
customer_question: Is this suitable for outdoor use?
scenario_label: Customer asking about product suitability for a use case
tone_notes: Informative, reassuring

title: Storage capacity question
customer_question: How much can this hold?
scenario_label: Customer asking about product capacity or dimensions
tone_notes: Helpful, specific

title: Add item to order
customer_question: Can I add something to my order?
scenario_label: Customer wants to add items to an existing order
tone_notes: Helpful, check timing

title: Change item selected
customer_question: I selected the wrong item, can I change it?
scenario_label: Customer wants to swap one item for another before shipping
tone_notes: Solution-focused

title: Discount code didn't apply
customer_question: My discount code didn't apply at checkout
scenario_label: Promo code not working during checkout
tone_notes: Apologetic, solution-focused

title: Duplicate charge on statement
customer_question: I was charged twice for the same order
scenario_label: Duplicate payment detected
tone_notes: Urgent, immediate refund assurance

title: Password reset not received
customer_question: I'm not receiving the password reset email
scenario_label: Account access - password reset email not arriving
tone_notes: Technical troubleshooting

title: Third-party gifting praise
customer_question: I gifted this to my sister and she loves it
scenario_label: Customer reporting gift recipient loved the product
tone_notes: Warm, appreciative

title: Instagram unboxing with followers
customer_question: I featured you on my Instagram and my followers love it
scenario_label: Customer sharing brand on social media with followers
tone_notes: Grateful, encouraging

title: 5-star review notification
customer_question: I left you a 5-star review
scenario_label: Customer reporting they have left a positive review
tone_notes: Appreciative, warm

title: Pure satisfaction with no request
customer_question: I'm so happy with my purchase
scenario_label: Customer expressing satisfaction with no request attached
tone_notes: Warm, genuine appreciation

title: Social handle provided
customer_question: Here's my Instagram handle if you want to see my post
scenario_label: Customer providing social media handle for brand engagement
tone_notes: Engaged, grateful

title: NEGATIVE: International shipping should NOT be brand_feedback
customer_question: NEGATIVE_EXAMPLE: Do you ship to New Zealand?
scenario_label: NEGATIVE: This is a pre-purchase question, not brand feedback
tone_notes: NEGATIVE EXAMPLE - Do not match

title: NEGATIVE: Address correction should NOT be brand_feedback
customer_question: NEGATIVE_EXAMPLE: I need to change my delivery address
scenario_label: NEGATIVE: This is order modification, not brand feedback
tone_notes: NEGATIVE EXAMPLE - Do not match

title: NEGATIVE: Add to order should NOT be brand_feedback
customer_question: NEGATIVE_EXAMPLE: Can I add to my order?
scenario_label: NEGATIVE: This is order modification, not brand feedback
tone_notes: NEGATIVE EXAMPLE - Do not match

title: NEGATIVE: Follow-up should NOT be brand_feedback
customer_question: NEGATIVE_EXAMPLE: Following up on my previous email
scenario_label: NEGATIVE: This is other_uncategorized, not brand feedback
tone_notes: NEGATIVE EXAMPLE - Do not match

title: NEGATIVE: Payment dispute should NOT be damaged_missing
customer_question: NEGATIVE_EXAMPLE: Payment taken but no order confirmed
scenario_label: NEGATIVE: This is account billing, not damaged/missing
tone_notes: NEGATIVE EXAMPLE - Do not match

title: NEGATIVE: Login issue should NOT be damaged_missing
customer_question: NEGATIVE_EXAMPLE: Can't log in to my account
scenario_label: NEGATIVE: This is account billing, not damaged/missing
tone_notes: NEGATIVE EXAMPLE - Do not match

title: NEGATIVE: Discount code should NOT be order_modification
customer_question: NEGATIVE_EXAMPLE: Discount code didn't apply at checkout
scenario_label: NEGATIVE: This is account billing, not order modification
tone_notes: NEGATIVE EXAMPLE - Do not match

title: NEGATIVE: Gift advice should NOT be partnership
customer_question: NEGATIVE_EXAMPLE: I'm looking for a gift for my sister
scenario_label: NEGATIVE: This is pre-purchase, not partnership
tone_notes: NEGATIVE EXAMPLE - Do not match

title: Where is my parcel
customer_question: Where is my parcel?
scenario_label: Customer asking for parcel location with tracking
tone_notes: Provide tracking, helpful

title: Delivered to wrong address
customer_question: The courier delivered my order to the wrong address
scenario_label: Courier delivery error - delivered to incorrect address
tone_notes: Urgent, action-oriented

title: Gift timing urgency
customer_question: My gift needs to arrive by Friday, can you guarantee delivery?
scenario_label: Time-sensitive gift order with delivery deadline
tone_notes: Reassuring, specific about timing

title: Tracking not updating
customer_question: My tracking hasn't updated in days
scenario_label: Tracking link stuck or not updating
tone_notes: Investigate, follow up with courier

title: Delivery delay notification
customer_question: I received a delay notification, when will my order arrive?
scenario_label: Customer responding to shipping delay notification
tone_notes: Apologetic, give new timeline

title: Return without receipt
customer_question: I received this as a gift but don't have the receipt, can I return it?
scenario_label: Gift return without receipt - adds complexity
tone_notes: Helpful, explain gift return policy

title: Refund status follow-up
customer_question: What's the status of my refund?
